## Description
<!-- Provide a brief description of your changes -->

## Type of Change
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix
- [ ] ✨ New feature
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] ♻️ Code refactoring
- [ ] 🔧 Configuration/tooling
- [ ] 🎨 UI/Style improvement

## Related Issues
<!-- Link to related issues: closes #123, relates to #456 -->

## Testing
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Manual testing completed
- [ ] Edge cases considered

## Code Standards
- [ ] Code follows project conventions
- [ ] ESLint checks pass (`npm run lint`)
- [ ] No `console.log()` left in code (only `console.error()` and `console.warn()` allowed)
- [ ] Comments added for complex logic
- [ ] No secrets or API keys accidentally included

## Frontend (if applicable)
- [ ] Components properly documented
- [ ] Props validation in place
- [ ] React hooks used correctly
- [ ] Accessibility considered

## Backend (if applicable)
- [ ] Code follows C# naming conventions
- [ ] Public APIs documented
- [ ] Error handling included
- [ ] Tests use mocked HttpClient
- [ ] No hardcoded configuration

## Coverage
- [ ] Test coverage maintained or improved
- [ ] Coverage meets 60% threshold (`npm run test:coverage`)
- [ ] Critical paths have 80%+ coverage

## Security
- [ ] No hardcoded secrets
- [ ] No SQL injection vulnerabilities
- [ ] Input validation applied
- [ ] Dependencies checked for vulnerabilities (`npm audit`, `dotnet list package --vulnerable`)

## Performance
- [ ] No obvious performance regressions
- [ ] Large data sets handled efficiently
- [ ] API response times acceptable

## Documentation
- [ ] README updated (if applicable)
- [ ] Inline comments added for complex logic
- [ ] PR description explains "why" not just "what"

## Checklist
- [ ] All CI checks pass
- [ ] Ready for review
- [ ] No WIP (work in progress) code included

## Additional Notes
<!-- Add any additional context or information reviewers should know -->
